summary: maplibre-gl-vue3 repackages maplibre-gl without Vue 3 bindings
details: The package name `maplibre-gl-vue3` suggests a Vue 3 wrapper/integration for maplibre-gl, but the shipped contents are a byte-level repackaging of the upstream `maplibre-gl` library (v5.24.1) with no Vue integration. `package.json` sets `main` to `dist/maplibre-gl.js` (the upstream UMD bundle) and copies the upstream `description` field verbatim ("BSD licensed community fork of mapbox-gl..."). The manifest also lists the package itself (`maplibre-gl-vue3`) as one of its own runtime dependencies, which is a malformed manifest shape. No lifecycle scripts, no injected payload, and no exfiltration/RCE mechanisms were observed in the bundle — installer harm is limited to identity/name confusion (developers looking for a Vue 3 integration receive the raw maplibre-gl library under a misleading name).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
